line_items.purpose: tau2-bench retail evaluation — 150 simulations x 5 trials
Production pipeline — enrichment + email generation (20 benchmark interactions)
line_items.basis: EMAIL_MODEL=qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-thinking; ENRICHMENT_MODEL=qwen/qwen3.5-9b. Production cost not metered during challenge week — no OpenRouter invoice line generated.

line_items.purpose: Transactional email send (dev mode — all routed to SINK_EMAIL)
line_items.basis: Free tier (3,000 emails/month). 0 real recipient emails sent.

line_items.purpose: CRM contact creation and timeline events via MCP
Discovery call booking (dev mode returns mock booking)
LLM observability — traces for all enrichment + email generation spans

note_on_production_cost: Production pipeline ran qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-thinking (email) and qwen/qwen3.5-9b (enrichment). No OpenRouter invoice was generated for production interactions during the challenge week. Switching to a paid flash-class model (Claude Haiku 4.5 or equivalent) would cost approximately $0.01–$0.03 per lead at current token volumes.
qualification_rate_basis: 11 of 20 benchmark interactions produced a classified ICP segment; remainder classified as abstain (insufficient signals) or skipped by bench gate
cost_per_qualified_lead_basis: tau2-bench proxy: $0.0199 / 0.55 qualification rate = $0.0362. Rounds to <$0.05 on free tier. Challenge target: <$5.
